scores.correctness_notes: Covers modern platforms/base syntax and legacy bases approach correctly, but critically misrepresents multi-base builds as using YAML multi-document syntax (---), which is wrong. The actual multi-base format embeds the base in the platform key (e.g., ubuntu@22.04:amd64:) without top-level base key. Also omits supported bases list (ubuntu@22.04, ubuntu@24.04, ubuntu@24.10, ubuntu@25.04, almalinux@9).
scores.hallucination_notes: The YAML multi-document syntax (using --- separator) for multi-base builds does not exist in charmcraft — this is a fabricated pattern. The real approach uses base-embedded platform keys like ubuntu@22.04:amd64: as shown in the gold standard.
